docs/decision-log.md:29:| 2026-06-09 | **Operating doctrine (owner-set)** — Codex pinned to `gpt-5.5`/`xhigh`, reserved for review/rescue/adversarial, kept within the $20 plan; Gemini (gated) = current latest model, freshness-checked at use, spend <$5; public docs name a credible free/OSS alternative per paid tool; every handoff ends with a paste-ready resume prompt; validate every recommendation for correctness + bias and Codex-cross-check consequential ones | Accepted (owner-directed) | Owner-set standing policy controlling model/cost, public-doc honesty, handoff continuity, and recommendation quality; refines `RULES.md` (RULES wins on conflict); Codex model/effort verified at `~/.codex/config.toml` | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine, 2026-06-09) |
docs/decision-log.md:34:| 2026-06-11 | **Free-first runtime stack (owner-directed)** — every product-runtime tool/service defaults to free / free-tier / self-hostable OSS (no paid subscriptions, no metered overage); the **only paid runtime exception is the Gemini API**: best current production model, freshness- and price-checked at use, **$5 hard total budget**, spend tracked; free-tier limits verified against official pricing at selection time; a tool whose free tier can't carry the slice routes back to the owner. Dev tooling (Claude/Codex) unchanged on existing plans | Accepted (owner-directed) | Owner set the cost rule 2026-06-11 ("use free tool or free tier tools; except Gemini API calls use best model, under $5 is the budget"); sharpens the 2026-06-09 doctrine (Gemini <$5 + free-alternative naming) into the default runtime selection rule; supports the cost-aware public posture (RULES §3 cost-control, §6 verification) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Free-first runtime stack); roadmap Phases 3–6 free routes (Slack free tier/Mattermost · Resend free tier/SMTP · Supabase free tier/self-hosted Postgres · self-hosted n8n · Langfuse/Phoenix) |
docs/decision-log.md:35:| 2026-06-11 | **Project identity = prototype run on demand, not an operated service (owner-directed)** — ActivationOps AI is a prototype solving a use case, executed episodically (demo / eval / walkthrough runs); **no 24/7 operation**, hosting, uptime, deployment, or ops requirements as goals; production-scale operation stays a documented Enterprise Expansion Path, never a build target without owner re-decision; any approved future integration phase = transient flow demonstration, not standing infrastructure | Accepted (owner-directed) | Owner clarification 2026-06-11 ("This won't be running 24/7. It is a project or prototype solving a use case"); prevents ops-engineering drift; explains why free tiers suffice (episodic load); consistent with RULES §3 (cost control before scale) and the playbook's Enterprise Expansion Path (document, don't build) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Project identity); `docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md` (Enterprise Expansion Path) |
docs/decision-log.md:36:| 2026-06-11 | **Effort policy (owner-directed)** — every stage runs at **MAX effort** (full reasoning depth + full rigor), and the effort level is **declared per stage** in the Professional Process Applied block; standing policy common across all the owner's projects (recorded here for this repo); depth maximal, ceremony proportional per RULES §15 anti-bloat | **Superseded** (2026-06-12 owner correction — effort AUTO-ADJUSTS by task for both Claude and Codex; see the 2026-06-12 effort row; the per-stage declaration survives) | Owner directive 2026-06-11 ("dont forget to tell to what effort level to be kept. for each stage right now it is in max this is common for all"); aligns with Codex pinned `xhigh` and the Gemini best-model policy | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Effort & project isolation) |
docs/decision-log.md:37:| 2026-06-12 | **Adopt the owner's standing PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS policy** (`~/claude-os/docs/PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS.md`, owner-set 2026-06-12; owner directed this repo to take the update) — project mapping: (1) **tooling ladder** sharpens the free-first doctrine: native/free/open first · self-hosted-over-cloud default (n8n/Supabase class) · free-tier ceilings + fallbacks recorded · trials = evaluation only, never load-bearing · only-paid-exists → build free equivalent or defer; (2) **product LLM = Gemini** with routing designed inside the ~$5 cap: free tier for dev/test → Flash-class for volume → Pro-class only where genuinely needed (refines "best current model, <$5" — latest generation, tier by need); (3) **data-source biases** (latest/fresh · open · live · hybrid) are judgment-weighted preferences, NOT hard gates — this project's Codex-no-shipped live-runtime-API decision stands as the documented trade-off (determinism/golden-eval/public-honesty win); (4) **vetting rule:** every stack/tool/dataset decision verified against live, date-anchored sources (extends RULES §6); (5) **completion deliverable:** a SPECIFIC expansion & adoption path is part of "done" (folded into Phase 7); (6) **location anchors:** owner-side = US; the product's target market = explicit intake question, never assumed | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-12: "some updates has been done so check it out in claude os") | Standing cross-project resourcing constraints; the doc itself binds "every user project"; consistent with the 2026-06-11 free-first + prototype-identity rows; RULES.md still wins on any conflict | `~/claude-os/docs/PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS.md`; `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine addendum); `docs/roadmap.md` (Phase 7); `docs/open-questions.md` (target-market intake); `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` (S3 note) |
docs/decision-log.md:39:| 2026-06-11 | **Project goal / Definition of Done RATIFIED (owner GO)** — ActivationOps AI is **done** when a company-agnostic, de-branded public repo holds a prototype **run on demand** (no 24/7 operation) that solves **merchant onboarding & activation for a local-commerce delivery marketplace** (specific use case kept; fictional platform name pending trademark check) end-to-end: deterministic triage · bounded LLM drafting (best current Gemini, **<$5 total**) measured against the **v2 baseline** · human approval gate · idempotent simulated sends · a walkthrough demo showing the **hold / reject / send** paths · public docs honestly separating built vs designed · $0 runtime beyond Gemini · max effort every stage *(effort sub-clause later superseded — auto-adjust-by-task, 2026-06-12 row; the DoD itself stands)*. **= T-003 → Phase 3 → Phase 7 (pulled forward).** **Phases 4–6 = optional roadmap**, re-decided by the owner only after Phase 3 evidence (if ever built: transient demonstrations, not standing infrastructure); **L4 stays the designed ceiling in the narrative, never the completion bar** | Accepted (owner GO 2026-06-11: "Your call. continue" — use-case specificity delegated to Claude and exercised as the local-commerce marketplace framing; recommendation Codex-cross-checked same day, AGREE ×4) | Closes the project-level DoD/audience question open since 2026-06-01; anchors the recorded objective ("enterprise-grade, interview-defensible AI build"); ends the goal oscillation (agentic dropped 06-01 → reinstated 06-09 → reassessed 06-11); consistent with the prototype-identity + free-first doctrine | Task-log 2026-06-11 (goal reassessment); `docs/roadmap.md` (DoD block); `docs/open-questions.md` (resolved) |
docs/decision-log.md:46:| 2026-06-12 | **Product target market = US** — the standing-constraints intake question answered by the owner; anchors S3 v2 dataset realism (US states, phone/email formats), the Source-Intake pick, and the Phase-7 expansion/adoption framing | Accepted (owner, 2026-06-12 grill) | Matches the existing v1 dummy data and the portfolio audience; proposed default confirmed | `docs/open-questions.md` (intake Q3); `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` draft 3 (S3) |
docs/decision-log.md:50:| 2026-06-19 | **PIVOT — rebuild as a real, industry-ADOPTABLE, deployed AI product (owner full-liberty GO; plan approved)** — supersede the Python-CLI-only delivery shape (and Phase-7-as-docs/walkthrough) with a single-stack **Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind + React on Vercel (free tier)** product that **ports** the proven deterministic core (Python kept tagged `v1-python-prototype` + as a differential-test oracle), integrates **real bounded Gemini** (schema-constrained · claims-gatekeeper · mode taxonomy · cost ledger <$5 · model preflight), makes the **eval harness the spine** (scores real LLM output + a real reproducible caught failure), runs on **HYBRID data** (real open-source entities + synthetic activation overlay, free, honestly labeled, untrusted-real-data sanitized), and ships a **deployed desktop review console**. Benchmark = **adoption-grade** (a real marketplace could inherit + run it). **Equal weight** to Strategy/Ops/BA judgment AND deep applied-AI; answers the full **why-chain** (each why names the rejected alternative + its cost); **universally legible**; **desktop-only**; job search in the **background**. **Keeps** the merchant-activation use case, deterministic-first→bounded-LLM order, eval-first, free-first (Gemini sole paid <$5), prototype-not-service (deploy = transient demonstration, not 24/7 ops), and the honesty posture. **Refines/supersedes the 2026-06-11 DoD (row 39) at the deliverable level** (CLI+docs → deployed product); T-003 S1/S2/S3/S5 fold into the rebuild's Phases A–B; `docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md` + `PLAN.md` are superseded as the active plan. Execution via `/autopilot`; owner-gated stops = commits · dataset-source check-in · platform-name · public posting · irreversible/external. | Accepted (owner full liberty + plan approved 2026-06-19) | This session's research (2026 US AI demand — deployed+real-LLM+evals are table-stakes, evals the breakout skill; competitive gap — "AI for merchants" commoditized, the governed stalled-long-tail-activation vertical is the wedge) + 4-agent positioning validation (which also corrected stale legal anchors: EU AI Act high-risk→Art 50 transparency; FTC *Air AI*→*Moffatt v. Air Canada*; governance-as-headline→domain-led) + advisor cross-checks; the offline Python artifact read as "designed, not built" | `~/.claude/plans/gentle-forging-starlight.md` (canonical goal · DoD · phases · blindspots) |
docs/decision-log.md:56:| 2026-06-22 | **Deepen to a calibrated semantic LLM-judge + roadmap production (owner direction)** — build a reference-grounded per-claim entailment faithfulness judge (Flash-Lite, secondary control after the deterministic gatekeeper) calibrated on a labeled gold set, eval-locked; AND a trigger-bound production roadmap. Keeps prototype-not-service + free-first (<$5); execution deferred to a fresh session per the committed, research-grounded plan. | Accepted (owner, 2026-06-22 — "deepen now + roadmap production"; "fresh session w/ committed plan") | Closes the documented Phase-B gap (undeclared prose fabrications the forward-checker can't catch); design grounded in current best-practice (research-specialist digest, cited) — judge on Flash-Lite to mitigate Flash-judges-Flash self-preference + for cost; gold-set negatives mined from the real live-run snapshot. | `docs/plan-semantic-judge-and-deepening.md` (the committed build plan + cited digest) |
docs/decision-log.md:57:| 2026-06-22 | **Project goal CONFIRMED = portfolio/capability SHOWCASE, not a venture; differentiation reframed to VERIFICATION RIGOR** — resolves the goal-fork surfaced by a 3-thread market-validation research pass (research-specialist ×2 + opportunity-finder, 2026 sources). Findings: (1) our exact use case is **already shipped** — DoorDash "AI-Powered Marketer" auto-generates merchant outreach (2026-05-04); (2) the **horizontal AI trust-layer is crowded + consolidating** (5 acquisitions in ~12mo; faithfulness *scoring* commoditized; the policy→block→HITL→audit gate already ships — Galileo/Fiddler/NeMo) → standalone-product-for-this-class = non-viable; (3) the **one differentiated, still-open seam** = deterministic-first per-claim verification against the **structured system-of-record (the DB row), not retrieved RAG context** — exists only as a *pattern*, never a bound product. Owner chose **portfolio** over venture/hybrid, so DoorDash shipping the use case reads as **PROOF the problem is real**, not a threat. **Binding honesty fix:** frame differentiation as "we verify AI claims against the source of truth," **never** "no one automates this" (false as of 2026-05). No scope change — confirms the existing roadmap (semantic judge → UI redesign → deploy) and sharpens its positioning. | Accepted (owner, 2026-06-22 — AskUserQuestion: "Portfolio showcase") | Same facts read as "pivot" or "proof" depending on the goal; the canonical project identity (prototype-not-service, job-search-background) is portfolio, so the venture verdict doesn't apply; the structured-SOR faithfulness spine (= the greenlit semantic judge) is the differentiation and holds under the chosen goal | `docs/research/market-validation-2026-06-22.md` (dated digest + citations); reaffirms row 35 (prototype identity), row 56 (semantic judge) |
docs/decision-log.md:62:| 2026-06-25 | **Reversal (c): "integrations deferred" → unblocked as a TRANSIENT DEMO** — Slack approval + email send + n8n (A5), **no send without human approval**, idempotent + suppression-aware; durable deliverable = a **recorded walkthrough** (public artifact stays REPLAY-only). *original:* 2026-06-01 "V1 = single offline slice; integrations deferred"; *why changed:* the `RULES.md` §3 rule-8 precondition ("no live integration until the offline thin slice is complete **and reviewed**") is now **SATISFIED** — the offline slice is built + reviewed + a live Gemini run executed under it; *impact:* A5 is a transient demonstration, not standing infra (prototype-not-service held). **Framed as scoped unblocking, NOT a reversal of `RULES.md` §3** (Codex #8) — only the decision-log deferral row is reversed | Accepted (owner 2026-06-25) | The §3 gate opened by its own terms; reframing it as a constitution reversal would invent a conflict and weaken governance | 2026-06-01 "integrations deferred" row; `RULES.md` §3 rule 8; `docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md`; `docs/reviews/codex-2026-06-25-multiagent-pivot.md` (#8) |
docs/decision-log.md:63:| 2026-06-25 | **Tech-stack selection = best-fit, cost-aware (owner-directed)** — refines the 2026-06-11 free-first runtime rule (row 34) + the 2026-06-12 tooling-ladder (row 37) from a **hard gate** into a **default bias**: pick the tool that best serves the project goal (a credible, modern, bounded multi-agent AI capability, company-agnostic); free / free-tier / self-hostable stays the cost-aware default, but a clearly-better **paid / enterprise-grade** tool is allowed where it **materially serves the goal**, with a named justification + live freshness/price check (`RULES.md` §6) + (consequential picks) **owner sign-off + Codex cross-check**. **Unchanged:** Gemini = cost-tracked LLM, **$5 hard total**; honesty/simulated labels; **prototype-not-service**; dev tooling (Claude/Codex) on existing plans. The near-term scope/sequencing cap (P3-calibration + A1 + A2; row 59 / Codex #4 / AM-6) is **NOT** reopened — this widens *which* tools may be chosen at A3+, not *when* | Accepted (owner-directed 2026-06-25, AskUserQuestion: "Best-fit, cost-aware") | Owner: *"dont limit to free tech stack to what i mentioned … according the goal of the project align it"* — the strict free-only tightening under-served the elevated multi-agent goal; realigns this project to the owner's global "free-first **with the enterprise path alongside**" posture; cost-discipline preserved as the default bias (RULES §3 cost-control, §6 verification still apply) | `CLAUDE.md` (Operating Doctrine — Tech-stack selection); supersedes the hard-gate reading of rows 34 + 37; `docs/plan-multi-agent-execution.md` Constraint #1 (patched) |
docs/decision-log.md:74:| 2026-07-02 | **PIVOT RE-OPENED (owner-directed 2026-07-01/02) + FIXED OBJECTIVE settled + research stage EXECUTED.** The owner re-opened the 2026-06-22 goal-fork on its pivot side: find a real, high-value, **structurally** underexplored problem in the DoorDash/Uber Eats/Grubhub-class US delivery-marketplace industry (company-agnostic) and solve it with a vertical AI solution at **adoption-grade prototype** standard ("could be adopted" = the quality bar, owner: metaphorical, not a literal sales target). Owner-settled via AskUserQuestion: **end goal = showcase-first, venture-ready** · **reuse = prefer the verification spine, evidence can override** · adopter = an output of the research per candidate. Constraints unchanged (prototype-not-service, $5 cap, honesty rules). Research executed same session (2 quarantined threads, ~100 sources checked): ranked digest in `docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md` — #1 fee-statement/fee-cap audit (LEAD-POTENTIAL), #2 cross-surface menu/agent-data truth verification (LEAD-POTENTIAL, early), composite "marketplace truth-audit layer" recommended by both threads; H1 dispute automation CONTESTED (Loop $14M Series A + DoorDash ToS prohibition), H2 refund-abuse AVOID (structural test fails). **The pivot DIRECTION is NOT decided** — next gate = owner picks a candidate → plan/roadmap (Codex cross-check before the pick is treated as decided). SLICE-2 close-out (live re-run) SUSPENDED by this redirect — its uncommitted diff untouched on disk. | Accepted (owner directive + AskUserQuestion answers 2026-07-02; research artifact = evidence, not a decision) | Owner-triggered goal redefinition per the front-door triage (half-done → owner asks → re-derive, surface, owner fixes); consequential scope work routed through plan-mode approval + the fixed-objective questions before research ran | `docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md`; plan `~/.claude/plans/peppy-honking-sparkle.md`; CURRENT_TASK/HANDOFF top blocks |
docs/decision-log.md:78:| 2026-07-02 | **INTENT REFINEMENT (owner) + USE-CASE BACKLOG created.** The owner confirmed the standing intent behind the pivot: **demonstrate deep AI proficiency to the delivery-marketplace industry by benefiting it** (platforms = indirect beneficiaries — efficiency/operational/cost — even where the direct customer is the merchant side; positioning frame = "marketplace integrity infrastructure"), **company-agnostic**, targeting problems the industry faces NOW, built **lean / optimized / structured** (existing-system redesign in scope), and **the local repo may be restructured according to the fixed goal** (execution of any restructure still gated at the plan/build stages). All surfaced use cases saved as an independent, future-workable backlog: `docs/research/use-case-backlog.md` (UC-1..UC-9 + re-check triggers). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-02) | Preserves the research value beyond the picked composite; the intent frame steers plan-stage positioning (cooperative story leads via UC-2, revenue story via UC-1) | `docs/research/use-case-backlog.md`; `docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:82:| 2026-07-02 | **REFRAME ACCEPTED FOR PLANNING (owner, on Claude's independent-judgment proposal): UC-2's lead artifact = an OPEN ACP/UCP conformance + truth-audit toolkit ("the truth layer for agentic commerce") + a self-referential demo (a real AI agent caught ordering from a deliberately-drifted synthetic menu) — instead of a merchant-facing prototype SaaS.** Why: makes "adoption-grade" literally achievable (open tooling the ecosystem can run the week it ships, vs simulated adoption); deletes UC-2's #1 named killer (surface-access/ToS — we validate feeds handed to us + public specs + our own sandboxes, no marketplace scraping); rides a short real timing window (ACP feed spec live; UCP announced 2026-07-01; **no conformance/verification tooling exists** — UC-7 promoted from horizon into UC-2's lead artifact); maximal intent-frame fit (cooperative, platforms/OpenAI/Google/Square all benefit); spec churn flips from risk to moat (validator-author pattern: SSL Labs / npm-audit → the commercial audit layer on top). UC-1 (fee-cap audit) unchanged as module two — same verifier, money-lines domain. **PLUS standing plan-stage directives (owner, same session):** (a) Claude holds the independent-judgment license through research/planning until build — proactively add own suggestions; at build, flip to spec-adherence + escalate; (b) **LEGIBILITY is a hard design constraint on the artifact itself** ("not complex outwards": one-command validator, one-page drift report, a demo needing no explanation — complexity spent inside, simplicity delivered outside); (c) data strategy deliberately spans **free/open + live (ToS-clean only: public specs, own sandbox accounts, merchant-permissioned) + hybrid + synthetic**, with edge-case coverage as an **enumerated taxonomy + measured eval coverage** — never an "all edge cases" overclaim; (d) everything free/free-tier except Gemini (≤$5 hard cap); the demo agent is a demonstration actor — scripted/simulated (labeled) or Gemini-driven within the cap — never Claude/Codex as runtime. | Accepted for planning (owner 2026-07-02 — "your suggestions looks good"; ratification unchanged: council deep-validation + mandatory Codex cross-check + owner GO on the plan) | Claude-originated reframe under the owner's explicit "independent judgment, full liberty" ask; it amends "adoption = metaphorical quality bar" toward partly-literal OSS adoption — surfaced as an owner-level objective change, owner accepted; all honesty/cost/prototype-not-service constraints hold | `docs/research/use-case-backlog.md` (UC-2 + UC-7); `docs/PLAIN-ENGLISH.md`; HANDOFF plan-stage prompt (amended) |
docs/decision-log.md:86:| 2026-07-02 | **Standing directive: DOCUMENTATION STANDARD adopted (floor, not ceiling) — two registers, one truth.** The owner refined the legibility directive: plain must never mean *diluted* — documentation uses the correct technical/business/operational terminology (professional register leads), decoded via first-use explanations + a shared **`docs/GLOSSARY.md`** (created, 21 seed terms); `PLAIN-ENGLISH.md` stays the pure lay entry-point; divergence between registers = a defect. Docs are professionally crafted — visual+text pairing, language flow, tone, nuance, **emotional resonance carried by narrative structure (SCQA), never hype** — built progressively as the project goes. Named frameworks adopted as the floor: **Diátaxis** (doc architecture) · **Minto Pyramid/SCQA** (summaries + narrative) · **C4 model** (architecture visuals: context = lay view) · **ADRs** (existing) · **docs-as-code** (existing) · RULES §7 (Mermaid visual rules, existing). Same-breath maintenance: every meaningful change updates technical docs + PLAIN-ENGLISH + glossary + affected diagrams in the same commit. Full standard: `docs/documentation-standard.md`. | Accepted (owner 2026-07-02 — "floor requirements not ceiling"; frameworks = Claude's suggestion under the standing judgment license, accepted with the directive) | Owner intent: document "how a professional would document, with all fundamentals, methodologies, frameworks"; composes with (does not conflict with) the "not complex outwards" directive — professional ≠ impenetrable, plain ≠ shallow | `docs/documentation-standard.md`; `docs/GLOSSARY.md`; `docs/PLAIN-ENGLISH.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:88:| 2026-07-02 | **Standing scope constraint (owner): DESKTOP WEB ONLY — no mobile version.** Any UI surface the pivot build ships (validator report viewer, demo walkthrough, console) targets desktop web browsers alone; no mobile layouts, no responsive-mobile requirement, no native/mobile deliverable. Re-affirms and extends the original roadmap's "mobile/responsive out of scope" line to the reframed UC-2/UC-1 build. Folds into the plan-stage success criteria as a hard constraint (CLI/validator artifacts unaffected — they have no UI). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-02 — mid-plan-stage directive: "no mobile version just desktop web version alone is enough") | Keeps the build lean per the owner's lean/optimized/structured intent; desktop is the demo/eval surface for a prototype-not-service | Plan-stage doc (Phase 4 success criteria); `CURRENT_TASK.md` |
docs/decision-log.md:96:| 2026-07-03 | **FABLE = FINAL JUDGE AT EVERY STAGE + STANDING FULL-LIBERTY LICENSE (owner-directed).** Owner (verbatim core): "ensure all the capabilities are utilized fully post check … take full liberty and full permission … reconcile and do it. also fable be final judge in all stages final call. Fable its own judgement in … giving the standout project." Rulings: (1) every gate verdict, reconciliation, and stage-exit call is made on the Fable seat (fable-override delegate, or a Fable session at the boundary) and is the FINAL CALL — primary-model-final binds to Fable specifically, even while Opus executes; Codex remains adversarial INPUT, never skipped, never blindly obeyed. (2) Fable holds a standing license to detect + apply blindspot fixes, refinements, and improvements on its own judgment toward a standout project — reversible/internal changes proceed without asking. (3) UNCHANGED hard stops: Gemini ≤$5 spend arming, deploy, public posting, git push, name adoption — owner word still required (RULES §12 wins over the license). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-03) | Concentrates judgment authority on the strongest seat while execution is delegated; matches the existing primary-model-final doctrine with the seat named explicitly | HANDOFF top block (ROUTE + JUDGE line); owner message 2026-07-03 |
docs/decision-log.md:100:| 2026-07-03 | **POST-CHECK ELEVATION MANDATE (owner-directed; extends the full-liberty + Fable-equivalence rows above).** Owner (verbatim core): "ensure all the capabilities are utilized fully post check. Use fable own judgement if any blindspots, fixes, refinements, improvements anything is take full liberty and full permission. Use your own judgement take it to next level in terms quality of work." Ruling: the per-slice loop is **check → judge → ELEVATE** — after the Fable-equivalence review returns PASS, the Fable seat runs an additional improvement pass on its own judgment: capability-utilization audit (is every relevant existing capability — eval harness patterns, red-green discipline, two-register docs, taxonomy coverage measurement, determinism/seeding — actually exercised, not just present?), blindspot hunt, and reversible refinements applied directly without asking. Quality target = standout, next-level work, not merely spec-satisfying. Hard stops unchanged (Gemini spend arming · deploy · public posting · push · name adoption). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-03) | Converts the standing license from permission into an obligation: elevation is a mandatory step of every slice's close-out, executed on the judgment seat | Owner message 2026-07-03; the two 2026-07-03 routing/judge rows above |
docs/decision-log.md:102:| 2026-07-03 | **DEPLOY DEFERRED UNTIL DESIGN IS FIXED (owner-directed).** Owner (verbatim): "defer deploy aspect, we will do it later once design is fixed." Deploy was already an owner-gated hard stop; this adds explicit sequencing — no deploy work (Vercel or otherwise), no deploy planning pressure in slice ordering, until the product/UI design is fixed and the owner re-opens it. The build program (W0/W1/P1 → M1) proceeds unaffected; deploy remains a documented later phase (prototype-not-service identity unchanged — any eventual deploy is a transient demonstration). | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-03) | Design-first sequencing; removes any implicit deploy deadline from the wedge slices | Owner message 2026-07-03; plan §5 (Pub/deploy stays owner-gated) |
docs/decision-log.md:104:| 2026-07-03 | **W1 EXECUTED INLINE ON THE FABLE SEAT — a recorded, bounded deviation from the delegation ruling (NO-WAIT applied).** Sequence: the W0+P1 Opus builders died on the shared subagent seat limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 7:40am (America/New_York)"), the owner confirmed reset, both relaunched and COMPLETED (W0 `1b04766`, P1 `da1e2e7`, each Fable-judged PASS + elevated). The W1 builder then died on a fresh limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 2:30pm (America/New_York)") at 10:18 ET — a ~4h wait. Under the NO-WAIT doctrine (wait-states → inline alternatives) + the standing full-liberty license (reversible internal work) + the owner's "resume", Fable executed W1 inline (`5a81440`), keeping the dead builder's sound partial work (C2 guard + category field). **Maker=judge mitigation, named:** (a) the M1 Codex cross-model batch reviews the whole wedge module (never skipped); (b) an independent acceptance-gate pass on W1 is a NAMED OBLIGATION once the subagent seat resets (extra rigor for this deviation, beyond the S-4 per-slice bar, which W1 met: verify green 409+5 + red-green ×4). Delegation resumes for subsequent slices when the seat allows. | Accepted (executed under standing owner rulings; surfaced in-session) | Two owner rulings collided (delegation vs NO-WAIT); the enumerated hard stops were untouched and the deviation is bounded to one slice with named compensating controls | `docs/reviews/w1-verify-evidence.log`; W1 commit `5a81440`; session transcript 2026-07-03 |
docs/decision-log.md:112:| 2026-07-04 | **F1b TAIL EXECUTED INLINE ON THE FABLE SEAT — recorded, bounded deviation (NO-WAIT, W1 precedent).** The delegated F1b builder died twice: (1) subagent seat limit (raw verbatim: "You've hit your session limit · resets 6pm (America/New_York)") → the owner's `resume` confirmed the retry; the SAME builder resumed with context intact and completed ALL build artifacts (58 tool uses); (2) then died on a raw "API Error: Overloaded" BEFORE the evidence log / slice record / same-breath docs. Fable executed that TAIL inline: RED-GREEN ×3 run independently by the reviewer (baseline pin — first mutation honestly recorded too-weak, strengthened; frozen-golden byte tamper; composition split-flip), evidence log, slice record, GLOSSARY +3, PLAIN-ENGLISH row. **Maker≠judge mitigation, named + advisor-ruled (frontier-advisor pre-wrap): the M2 cross-model ceremony runs NOW over the offline F1 module (`896ab59`+`bda6314`) BEFORE any live leg; M2's scope explicitly enumerates (a) the verifier-core ClaimSource "classifier" shared-core touch and (b) the reviewer-executed red-green runs as items Codex must independently re-verify; the owner-gated live classifier run is BLOCKED on M2 SHIP.** | Accepted (executed under standing owner rulings; surfaced at wrap) | Build artifacts stayed builder-authored + Fable-reviewed; the inline exposure is bounded to the documentation tail and compensated by the scheduled cross-model gate | `docs/reviews/f1b-slice-record.md`; commits `896ab59`/`bda6314`; frontier-advisor consult (session transcript 2026-07-04) |
docs/decision-log.md:114:| 2026-07-05 | **FOUR OWNER CALLS SETTLED in one word ("all four" — all recommendations adopted, surfaced 2026-07-04 post-M2): (1) LIVE CLASSIFIER RUN ARMED** — the owner-gated F1b live calibration run fires this session per `docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md` §3 (Groq free tier $0 · K=3 at temp 0 · TPD preflight + pacing · scored ONLY on the held-out 21-item test split vs the PRE-REGISTERED floors incl. the M2-amended ≥20/21 strictly-beats-baseline, tie = DEFER; any missed floor → the label honestly DEFERS, never re-run to green, never amend a floor post-hoc). **(2) GEMINI DEMO COLOR VARIANT DECLINED** — the ≤$0.50 non-load-bearing D1 cosmetic call is CLOSED (declined, revivable only by owner word); the transcript annotation slot stays. **(3) cargo/Rust TOOLCHAIN INSTALL ORDERED** (poppler precedent) — install locally + run the C5 oracle-agreement measurement (`npm run test:ucp-oracle`) against the official `ucp-schema` validator on the N=35 CI corpus; closes the past-horizon C5 open call. **(4) CORPUS LICENSE (O6) DEFERRED TO THE PUB GATE** — the license binds only at publication, which is itself owner-gated; decision consciously scheduled there, not dropped. | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-05 — "all four" adopting the four surfaced recommendations) | The four calls were surfaced exactly as the eleventh-session wrap directed; the owner's single-word adoption is faithful to each surfaced recommendation verbatim | HANDOFF top block (2026-07-04); `docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md` §3; this session's transcript |
docs/decision-log.md:116:| 2026-07-06 | **PUB RESEARCH LEGS EXECUTED INLINE ON THE FABLE SEAT (recorded, bounded deviation — NO-WAIT, W1/F1b precedent).** The delegated research subagents died on seat limits TWICE: first pair raw "You've reached your Fable 5 limit. Run /usage-credits to continue or switch models with /model."; owner's `resume` = the confirmed retry; the Sonnet-routed retry pair died raw "You've hit your session limit · resets 3:30am (America/New_York)" (the harness safety classifier was also briefly unavailable, blocking Bash). Conversion: S-11 name screen + news-moment scan + sanitization audit run INLINE with live web fetches (npm registry, GitHub API, NYC Rules/DCWP, web search), all dated 2026-07-06 in the records. The frontier-advisor consult itself SUCCEEDED pre-conversion (PROCEED-WITH-CONSTRAINTS; its ordering + sanitization-audit + README-replace + Codex-scope rulings all adopted). | Accepted (executed under standing owner rulings; surfaced at wrap) | Maker=judge on public prose is bounded by the Codex claims-vs-repo-truth batch + independent acceptance-gate that close the slice | This session's transcript; `docs/reviews/pub-verify-evidence.log` |
docs/decision-log.md:121:| 2026-07-06 | **EXTENSION GOAL FRAMING FIXED + SESSION WRAP (owner, fourteenth session; verbatim: "It is a personal project to demonstrate is the goal or the idea, accordingly wrap this session and with proper to do so that other account can continue with it.").** The agentic extension's purpose is a PERSONAL DEMONSTRATION project — showcase applied-AI / agentic-systems / AI-automation proficiency (target roles on record: AI Engineer, AI Specialist, Applied AI Engineer, AI Automation Specialist; boundary: NOT model development/training, NOT no-code-only ease; floor tools: Claude Code/Codex, n8n, MCP, Zapier-class — floor not ceiling). "Make it big" = breadth/depth of demonstrated proficiency across the four surfaces (agent crew · MCP server · Slack/email delivery · n8n workflow lane), NOT commercialization; prototype-not-service identity, honesty rules, $5 Gemini cap, and all hard stops carry over unchanged. NEXT STAGE = PLAN (fresh session, any account): declarative plan over the research digest → Codex cross-check → owner GO before any build. | Accepted (owner, 2026-07-06) | Locks the intent the extension is planned against, so the plan stage optimizes for demonstration value per unit of complexity | `docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md`; HANDOFF top block (fourteenth-session wrap) |
docs/decision-log.md:122:| 2026-07-07 | **AGENTIC EXTENSION BUILD ARMED (owner, fifteenth session; verbatim: "except design deploy, GO for it.").** Intent capture (Prompt Intake): GO on `docs/plan-agentic-extension.md` v1.0 — the full slice DAG (A0 registry → A1 MCP ∥ A2 crew ∥ A3 delivery → A4 n8n → AM ceremony); DESIGN and DEPLOY stay excluded by the owner's word (consistent with the standing 2026-07-03 design-first-then-deploy ruling). Owner-call dispositions under this GO: **O-A1 approved** (slice set + ordering as planned); **O-A2 = per-run arming stands** — L-1/L-2/L-3 each still need their own explicit word after their offline gates (RULES §3; nothing live fires under this GO); **O-A3 stays OPEN** (how the private repo is shown — owner act, blocks payoff not build); **O-A4 deferred to A4 entry** — docker is NOT installed on this machine (verified live 2026-07-07); the install ask will be surfaced at A4 entry per the poppler/cargo precedent, else A4 ships as the honest "workflow spec, not executed n8n lane"; **O-A5 dissolved for the build** — A3's email builder is provider-agnostic (RFC 5322 MIME payloads); provider naming binds only at the L-2 transient-demo arming; **O-A6 confirmed** (implementer lane; opus escalation for A2). Hard stops unchanged: live spend arming · deploy (design-first) · public flip · name adoption (S-11) · classifier retry (new pre-registration + word). | Accepted (owner GO 2026-07-07) | The GO fires exactly the plan the Codex cross-check reconciled; every live/irreversible act remains individually owner-gated | `docs/plan-agentic-extension.md` v1.0 §8; HANDOFF A0+A1 build prompt; this session's transcript |
docs/dual-model-workflow.md:66:- n8n added;
docs/dual-model-workflow.md:68:- Resend webhook added;
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:3:**Why this exists:** the owner's 2026-07-06 directive (decision-log, verbatim rows) extends the program: the showcase target is the **applied-AI / agentic-systems / AI-automation** proficiency companies currently hire for — agents, multi-agent systems, AI workflows, integrations (Slack, email, MCP, n8n/Zapier-class), built on frontier models — explicitly NOT model development/training, and NOT no-code-only toy use. Target roles named by the owner: AI Engineer, AI Specialist, Applied AI Engineer, AI Automation Specialist. This digest grounds the goal re-fix in live, dated sources per the standing rule (enterprise claims are researched, never memory-based).
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:13:- **"AI Automation Specialist"** is a real, distinct lane: builds AI-driven workflows integrating enterprise tools/APIs — "workflows too complex for a marketer with Zapier, but too small for a full-time engineering team" (mraiwala.com 2026 role comparison, practitioner tier). This matches the owner's n8n/Zapier/MCP framing exactly.
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:17:**Verdict:** what the owner described is professionally called **agent engineering / applied AI engineering**, building **agentic AI systems (vertical AI agents) and AI workflow automations**. The named tools (Claude Code/Codex, n8n, MCP, Zapier) are recognized floor-not-ceiling instruments of that lane.
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:35:- **n8n:** $180M Series C (Oct 2025), **$2.5B valuation**, >180k GitHub stars; serves as **the orchestration layer for SAP's Autonomous Enterprise platform** (n8n blog + chronexa/hatchworks 2026 guides; vendor+practitioner). Free self-hosted tier fits our cost rules.
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:36:- **The 2026 consensus pattern matches our thesis verbatim:** "n8n provides the reliable backbone for business processes — deterministic workflows that must execute consistently — while AI agents plug in at specific points where intelligence is needed" (superstackit/entrans 2026, practitioner tier).
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:46:- **Sierra** (customer-service agents; Bret Taylor): $950M raise May 2026, **$15.8B valuation**, $100M ARR in ~7 quarters. **Harvey** (legal agents): **$300M ARR, $11B valuation** Q2 2026 (saasmag/8seneca/foundevo 2026 roundups; magnitudes single-sourced per name, directionally consistent across all).
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:51:1. **The market name for what we're building:** an **agentic AI system / vertical AI agent** for marketplace truth & fee-compliance auditing, built by **applied-AI engineering** — with an **AI-workflow/automation** surface (n8n lane) covering the automation-specialist story.
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:52:2. **The architecture the evidence endorses is the one we can honestly build:** deterministic verified core (done) → agent crew orchestrating it as tools (to build) → MCP server exposure (to build) → Slack/email delivery (to build, offline-first per RULES §3) → observability/tracing + eval-in-CI (largely done; needs the agent-trajectory surface) → n8n workflow calling the same tools (optional lane, showcases the automation role).
docs/research/agentic-extension-research-2026-07.md:54:4. **Honesty constraints carry over unchanged:** agents recommend, the engine decides; simulated labels; no live integration until the offline slice + safety controls exist (RULES §3); live runs owner-gated; prototype-not-service.

docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md:112:`basic_service_fee` · `transaction_fee` · `enhanced_service_fee`) or the catch-all
docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md:151:| **Overall held-out precision (macro, across the 5 labels)** | **≥ 0.85** | macro-averaged across `delivery_fee / basic_service_fee / transaction_fee / enhanced_service_fee / not-a-permitted-fee` |
docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md:153:| **Per-class recall — `enhanced_service_fee` and `not-a-permitted-fee` specifically** | **≥ 0.80** | these are the two classes carrying the genuine relabeling/bundling drift the baseline MISSES (`relabel-test-2`, `bundle-test-2`) — the live run must show it actually resolves the cases the floor cannot, not just match it elsewhere |
docs/plan-f1b-classifier.md:193:but the run must still be scheduled as **one paced pass**, never assumed
docs/checklists/prevent-repeat-checklist.md:34:- [ ] Did we **avoid adding a new standing log** unnecessarily?
docs/research/use-case-backlog.md:62:- **Caveat (standing):** a platform (Galileo-class) could add it any quarter — the moat is domain depth + SOR integration, never the capability alone.
docs/reviews/gate-2026-07-06-pub-slice.md:21:3. Exact test counts in public prose = standing staleness trap; consider "740+" or a badge after publication → **surfaced at wrap**.
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:11:**The core intent is right; the planned 15-artifact structure is over-built and, as drafted, re-triggers the exact failure mode this project already diagnosed and rejected.** The traceability chain (messy idea → source scan → selection → alternatives → assumptions → approach → evidence → failure handling → reuse → expansion → handoff → approval) and "input can be messy, execution must be professional" are genuinely worth codifying — once, lightly. But shipping 15 standing artifacts (source scan log, evidence matrix, framework selection matrix, assumption log, failure taxonomy, etc.) on top of a 20-row dummy-data project would roughly double the governance surface and contradict the project's own commitments: `RULES.md` §13 (lightweight loop for offline work), §8 (don't let process overshadow product), the governance review's "governance has outgrown the product" finding, and `plan-reconciliation.md` §4 which **explicitly rejected a docs-first prerequisite gate**. T-001.5 as 15 artifacts is a new docs-first gate.
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:26:| Handoff-proof (#12) | `HANDOFF.md` + standing continuity procedures + §1 source-of-truth |
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:46:- **"Evidence matrix" (#3) as a standing artifact.** The test suite already is the evidence. A separate matrix duplicates it and will drift. → **Reject.**
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:49:- **"Source scan log" / "assumption log" as standing files (#2,#5).** Per-task standing logs go stale (the audit just proved docs drift). → **Reject as files; Add inside T-001.5 as short *sections/fields* in the task brief.**
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:53:This is the strongest structural concern. The repo already carries a heavy governance surface for a 20-row CSV: `RULES`, `AGENTS`, `CLAUDE`, `CODEX`, `dual-model-workflow`, `prevent-repeat-checklist`, 4 prompt templates, `decision-log`, `implementation-journal`, `task-log`, `open-questions`, `project-narrative`, `product-brief`, `data-audit`, `plan-reconciliation`, 3 reviews, 3 audits, review packets, visuals. Adding ~15 more would roughly **double** it. Existing overlaps already flagged: `implementation-journal` vs `decision-log` (both capture "why"); the 4 prompt templates; HANDOFF block accretion (cleaned in the audit). **Recommendation: the playbook must be net-neutral or net-negative on file count — absorb scattered guidance, don't add alongside it.** → **Must add before T-001.5: a "no new standing logs unless a rule requires one" constraint on the playbook itself.**
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:86:`HANDOFF.md` + standing procedures already serve Claude acct 1/2, CLI, Codex. Gaps: (a) the **git-state re-derivation requirement** (Must-add, above); (b) an explicit note that **ChatGPT web / external reviewers get the review-packet, not repo write access** (read-only context handoff). → git-state = **Must add before**; ChatGPT-handoff note = **Add inside T-001.5** (one line).
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:90:No current rule for "when you discover something that changes a prior decision, record it." Lightweight fix: **when new info invalidates a decision, log it in `decision-log.md` (or `open-questions.md`) and update the affected doc in the same task.** Reusable patterns → a one-line journal note. → **Add inside T-001.5** (2 lines). A standing "blindspot log" file is **Reject** (will go stale).
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:107:**Reject (process bloat):** standalone source-scan log, evidence matrix, framework selection matrix, standing assumption log, failure-taxonomy doc, standing blindspot log, "deep rationale always." **Defer to the stage that needs them:** integration security checklist (RLS/webhook-signing/least-privilege — already captured in `codex-initial-review` for later), operational cost/budget ledger rules, reliability/observability (retries/timeouts), the evaluation harness itself (that is T-002's candidate work, not a playbook rule), data-privacy/PII and accessibility (no real data / no UI yet).
docs/review-packets/T-001.5-standards-blindspot-review.md:114:- **Must add before T-001.5:** git-state re-derivation checklist step; "no new standing logs" constraint on the playbook.
docs/reviews/a2-verify-evidence.log:7:standing NO-WAIT rule (W1/F1b/Pub precedent). Mitigation for maker=judge: the
docs/review-packets/T-001-review-packet.md:9:Build the smallest credible offline workflow on the 20-row dummy CSV: ingest read-only → normalize to `out/merchants_v1.csv` → recompute risk → deterministic blocker diagnosis → human review queue → one stubbed structured draft behind guardrails → simulated send with idempotency → two append-only audit logs. Acceptance criteria are 18 tests (T1–T18, including a send-gate test and per-category guardrail fixtures). No external services, no credentials, no real sending.
docs/review-packets/T-001-review-packet.md:27:Live Supabase/n8n/Slack/Resend/Gemini; real credentials; real contacts/consent; real sending; per-step statuses/timestamps; delivery webhooks; outcome learning; cost ledgers; dashboards; 14-table schema; multi-channel; autonomous/agentic tool use.
docs/roadmap.md:7:> **⚠️ SUPERSEDED 2026-06-25 (owner-approved MULTI-AGENT PIVOT; Phase 0 gated):** the active goal is now a **bounded, HITL, eval-gated MULTI-AGENT verify-and-self-correct system** — the 2026-06-19 deployed product (with its judge · gatekeeper · eval harness · hybrid data · REPLAY) is **PROMOTED to the agents' tools, not rewritten**. Canonical: brief `~/.claude/plans/read-last-handoff-and-snappy-ripple.md` + execution spec `docs/plan-multi-agent-execution.md` (+ `docs/decisions/ADR-002-multi-agent-architecture.md`, decision-log 2026-06-25 rows). Phase 0 (mandatory Codex gate → **BLOCK → reconciled**; ADR-002 + 3 reversals + binding spec amendments) is nearly cleared; **committed near-term scope = P3-calibration + A1 + A2**, with A3/A4/A5/Track B as roadmap re-decided at the **A2 owner go/no-go**. The use case (merchant activation), deterministic-first→bounded-LLM, eval-first, free-first, prototype-not-service, and honesty all **carry forward**. The 2026-06-19 + earlier blocks are **historical**.
docs/roadmap.md:9:> **⚠️ SUPERSEDED 2026-06-19 (owner full-liberty GO; plan approved):** the canonical goal/DoD/phases now live in **`~/.claude/plans/gentle-forging-starlight.md`** (+ decision-log 2026-06-19 row) — a rebuild to a real, industry-adoptable, **deployed Next.js/TS desktop AI product** with real bounded Gemini + the eval harness as spine + hybrid data, equal-weight Strategy/Ops/BA & deep applied-AI, a full why-chain, universally legible, desktop-only. The **use case (merchant activation), deterministic-first→bounded-LLM, eval-first, free-first, prototype-not-service, and honesty all carry forward**; the *delivery shape* changed (Python-CLI+docs → deployed product). The block below is **historical**.
docs/roadmap.md:11:> **Definition of Done (ratified 2026-06-11, owner GO):** the project is **complete** when a company-agnostic public repo holds an **on-demand prototype** (a prototype solving a use case — no 24/7 operation, hosting, or ops) for **merchant onboarding & activation on a local-commerce delivery marketplace**: deterministic triage · bounded Gemini drafting (best current model, **<$5 total**; everything else free/free-tier) measured against the **v2 baseline** · human approval gate · idempotent simulated sends · a walkthrough showing the **hold / reject / send** paths (the **reject** path, until Phase 3's live model, is demonstrated via an **S4 eval-corpus replay** of a planted bad draft the guardrail catches — labeled as an injected case, since the deterministic stub can't produce a live rejection; see T-003·S3) · public docs honestly separating built from designed. **Done = T-003 → Phase 3 → Phase 7 (pulled forward).** Phases 4–6 are **optional roadmap**, re-decided by the owner only after Phase 3 evidence. Effort: **auto-adjusted by task** (ship-gating/high-risk → max; supersedes the earlier "max every stage" — `docs/decision-log.md` 2026-06-12).
docs/roadmap.md:20:- **Fully offline.** No live model, API, email, Slack/Resend/Supabase/n8n. The draft generator is a deterministic stub.
docs/roadmap.md:58:| Phase 4 | Human-in-the-Loop Delivery Workflow (live approval + send, idempotent) | L2 | **Optional** — owner re-decision after Phase 3; if built, a transient demo, never standing infra |
docs/roadmap.md:99:- **Specific expansion & adoption path (owner standing constraint, 2026-06-12 — part of "done," not boilerplate "future work"):** the final docs must name (a) **future expansion** — the concrete features/scale work needed past the shipped wedge, in order, each tied to its trigger (the existing Enterprise Expansion Path entries, made task-convertible); and (b) **adoption** — the explicit adopter class (here: merchant-onboarding/activation ops teams at local-commerce delivery marketplaces), what that adopter would require before using it (named compliance frameworks where applicable, the actual systems it must integrate with, security/support expectations, rollout path: one team → process-owner sign-off → org rollout). Every item names the thing, why, and roughly what it takes — if it can't become a task, it's not specific enough. The **product's target market is an intake question for the owner** (never defaulted to US; see `docs/open-questions.md`).
docs/roadmap.md:108:- No Slack/Resend/n8n/Supabase before the offline HITL workflow + safety controls are proven.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:48:- Google SRE — SLI/SLO/error budget — [sre.google SLO](https://sre.google/sre-book/service-level-objectives/), [sre.google error-budget policy](https://sre.google/workbook/error-budget-policy/)
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:61:- HITL workflow gate / control gate / approval gate / CI-CD `require_review` analogy — [IBM HITL](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/human-in-the-loop), [JumpCloud HITL gate](https://jumpcloud.com/it-index/what-is-a-human-in-the-loop-hitl-workflow-gate), [zapier HITL patterns](https://zapier.com/blog/human-in-the-loop/), [n8n HITL automation](https://blog.n8n.io/human-in-the-loop-automation/)
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:108:| Human-in-the-loop (HITL) / approval gate / control gate | Vendor+practice (IBM/Zapier/n8n) | **Use now** | Describe the send-gate as a HITL approval gate; use the CI-CD `require_review` analogy for laypeople | Phase 0 (done), all later | Implying a live human UI exists in V1 (it's a synthetic approval fixture) |
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:121:| Google SRE (SLI/SLO/SLA, error budget) | Tier1 (Google) | **Reject (now), reference later** | Needs a running production service with users/uptime — none exists | Far future (if ever hosted) | "SLO/error budget" on an offline sim = pure performance |
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:137:- Source: IBM, Zapier, JumpCloud, n8n · Type: vendor + practice · Date: 2026-06-02 · Means: human oversight at predefined high-risk points; a "workflow/control gate" pauses automation until a human approves (the CI-CD `require_review` pattern — "a human reviews the diff and clicks approve" before anything hits production). · Applicability: high — this is precisely the V1 send-gate (`approval_state`, High/ineligible held, never auto-sent). HITL is "most valuable… sending customer communications" — the project's exact domain. · Stage: Phase 0 (done); every later phase. · Borrow: "HITL approval gate"; the CI-CD analogy for laypeople. · Reject: implying a live approval UI (V1 uses a synthetic approval fixture). · Adapt: "human-review gate, proven in V1 without a live UI." · Validation: tests T8/T15/T17. · Risk: overstating the human tooling that exists.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:168:monitoring / observability / drift / feedback loop (Phase 2+, once a live model exists) · LLMOps (Phase 2+) · NIST GenAI-Profile risk mapping (Phase 2, light) · NIST SSDF practices (Phase 2+, when secrets/integrations land) · model versioning/lineage (Phase 3+, if model variants multiply) · webhook idempotency / suppression sync (Phase 4, Resend).
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:174:SRE / SLO / SLA / error budget (no production service) · DORA deploy-frequency/MTTR as a *current* claim (nothing deployed) · MLOps "training/retraining" (no training) · **agentic / autonomous agents** (explicitly dropped; contradicts a recorded decision) · **"production-grade / deployed to production / enterprise-scale / real impact"** (it's a simulation — the #1 inflated-portfolio claim) · "NIST-compliant," "SOC2/SLA-backed," "benchmark/SOTA" (uncertifiable / overstated at this scale).
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:184:- **Phase 4 — Human-in-the-loop delivery integrations (Slack approval, Resend send).** Real approval callbacks + real (still rate-limited/test-keyed) send with webhook idempotency + suppression. Full-loop risk.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:185:- **Phase 5 — Orchestration + monitoring (n8n) + outcome learning.** Workflow orchestration, error workflows, monitoring/observability, outcome-learning loop.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:216:- **Operating-a-system vocabulary with no system in operation:** SRE/SLO/error-budget, DORA, LLMOps monitoring/drift all presuppose a deployed service with users; using them now is performative.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:218:- **Process outgrowing product (the project's standing risk):** the packet itself must stay proportional — concise where possible, deep only where it changes a decision. The roadmap should be short and product-named — no framework-mapping section by default (at most a tiny artifact-tied terminology note), not a framework cathedral.
docs/review-packets/roadmap-lifecycle-applicability-review.md:246:2. **Future-only:** LLMOps monitoring/drift/feedback; NIST GenAI-Profile risk mapping (Phase 2); NIST SSDF practices (Phase 2+); model lineage; webhook idempotency/suppression.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-06-25-multiagent-pivot.md:19:| 4 | P1 | Scope too large for the locked **portfolio** showcase: Promptfoo, Langfuse, Docker, GH Actions, Slack, Resend, n8n, Track B — none in `package.json` yet. | **ACCEPT → FIX as *sequencing*, not cutting.** Committed near-term scope = **P3-calibration + A1 + A2**; A3/A4/A5/Track B stay **roadmap**, re-decided at the A2 go/no-go. Faithful to the owner's full vision *and* the discipline. | spec §6.6/§7 note; ADR-002 |
docs/implementation-journal.md:204:- Prevention step for the future: the marginal-value enforcement (gate-pass + faithful, live per item) is now a standing pattern for any reverse-of-an-axis judge — it makes "is this a pure residual?" a build-time assertion, not a claim.
docs/implementation-journal.md:322:- Reviewer notes: addresses both final-review P2s; no remaining Codex findings outstanding.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:64:**→ ActivationOps AI:** the Codex command surface was verified against the installed plugin (Tier 1, local), and vendor behaviors (Slack/Resend/Supabase/n8n/Gemini/Sheets) were cited to official docs in the initial review — generalize that, lightly.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:70:Search breadth (as the task warrants): official docs · standards bodies · vendor docs · mature open-source repos · GitHub issues / discussions / PRs / examples / changelogs · reputable company engineering blogs · product / UX / UI / design / portfolio references · LLMOps / MLOps / agent / eval / guardrail references · security / privacy / compliance references · automation/workflow examples (incl. n8n / Zapier / Pipedream / Slack / Resend / Supabase / Postgres / Gemini *when relevant*) · practitioner blogs & newsletters · Reddit & relevant subreddits · forums · YouTube videos/transcripts · Discord/community where accessible & appropriate · Stack Overflow / Q&A · recent changelogs / release notes / migration guides / issue threads.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:110:Applies to **every task**, not just research tasks. **Do not adopt anything by default** — no source, repo, template, workflow, tool, code/prompt/UI/design pattern, doc style, automation example, architecture, framework, or methodology — until it passes intake. Covers planning · code · data modeling · testing · AI prompts/agent patterns · guardrails · evals · security/privacy · n8n/Zapier/Pipedream · Slack/Resend/Supabase · Gemini/other models · Claude skills · Codex workflows · MCP · knowledge-management (e.g., Obsidian) · dashboards · UI/UX · visual design · README · portfolio case study · demo · docs · publishing.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:131:Applies to: Python · CSV/Google Sheets · Gemini · Supabase · n8n · Pipedream/Zapier (if considered) · Slack · Resend · Mermaid · dashboard/UI tools · testing tools · any future framework/library.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:137:Any model/API/platform/integration choice that affects implementation, cost, security, public claims, or future compatibility must include a freshness check: current supported version · official docs/changelog checked · deprecation/retirement risk · upgrade trigger · fallback · **date checked** · whether the uncertainty blocks action. Applies to Gemini, Supabase, n8n, Slack, Resend, Pipedream, Zapier, Claude Code, the Codex plugin, SDKs, APIs, and any future model/tool.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:207:Applies to docs, visuals, UI, README, dashboards, demos, walkthroughs, code comments, public presentation. The work should feel clear, modern, minimal, elegant, intuitive, professional, human-reviewed — easy for a layperson, deep enough for a technical reviewer. Modern patterns (cards, glassmorphism, gradients, micro-interactions, animation, diagrams, polished layouts) are **allowed when they improve clarity, navigation, storytelling, or understanding**. Avoid generic AI wording, fake polish, random decoration, confusing navigation, unsupported claims, overdesigned visuals, unreviewed AI output. **Make complex work understandable without making it shallow.**
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:211:Public docs, demos, dashboards, diagrams, and UI are reviewed for: clear hierarchy · readable language · clear labels · contrast · navigation clarity · layperson understanding · mobile/responsive awareness where relevant · visual restraint · no decorative complexity that hides meaning.
docs/enterprise-delivery-playbook.md:229:Do not create a new permanent tracking file unless it solves a repeated problem, replaces scattered process, or is required for a high-risk stage. Prefer updating the playbook, task log, decision log, implementation journal, handoff, and project state. Add a new standing file only if it clearly reduces confusion or improves quality.
docs/CASE-STUDY.md:50:- **Start simple; add complexity only when a real need justifies it.** No databases, no live email, no external services until the simple version has earned them.
docs/CASE-STUDY.md:74:- **Enterprise scale [PLANNED — see roadmap]:** the same guarantees on a real datastore (Postgres/Supabase) with migrations; bounded LLM drafting behind the guardrails; human approval (e.g. Slack) + rate-limited real delivery (e.g. Resend); orchestration + monitoring (e.g. n8n); multi-team/role separation. Each external system is added only once the simpler layer earns it — so an adopter stops at the scale they need.
docs/architecture/agentic-architecture-blueprint.md:130:| Orchestration | n8n (already OSS) | Temporal / plain code | delivery stable |
docs/architecture/agentic-architecture-blueprint.md:165:- Observability/state/delivery: **OSS-first** options keep the floor near $0; paid services are roadmap + gated.
docs/architecture/agentic-architecture-blueprint.md:172:- **Target (not built):** bounded Gemini drafting (L1) · live HITL delivery (L2) · policy-envelope auto-act (L3) · bounded planning/tool-use (L4) · OTel tracing · datastore · Slack/Resend/n8n. All **gated**, sequenced in `docs/roadmap.md`.
docs/architecture/agentic-architecture-blueprint.md:193:- **14.8 CI quality gate.** Eval-gated CI: tests + `scripts/eval.py` + the draft contract + guardrail probes pass before merge; **blocking pre-commit hooks** for secrets + git-state (T-003c); staged rollout + a scheduled post-launch review [S9, S10].
docs/visuals/v1-thin-slice-flow.mmd:5:%% Fully offline: no external services, no credentials, model call is stubbed.
docs/product-brief.md:23:- n8n self-hosted for orchestration.
docs/product-brief.md:25:- Resend for approved email delivery and webhook tracking.
docs/INTERVIEW-WALKTHROUGH.md:25:Runtime today: **Python standard library only** — no network, no AI call, no external services. The AI step is [PLANNED] for Phase 3, behind the existing guardrails.
docs/visuals/architecture.mmd:46:    ORCH["Orchestration (n8n)"]
docs/t002-slice-plan.md:313:Live Gemini or any API; secrets; Slack / Resend / Supabase / n8n; changes to source CSV; rewrites of T-001 tests; LLM output quality scoring; threshold “correctness” claims; performance benchmarks; CI; dashboard UI; enforcement hooks (separate approved task per [docs/research/source-intake-review.md](research/source-intake-review.md)).
docs/prompts/codex-plan-review-template.md:17:- Security / privacy risks — secrets, eligibility, data exposure, webhook trust.
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:11:- **Question:** how do we add the four owner-named surfaces (agent crew · MCP server · Slack/email delivery · n8n lane) without breaking the constraints that make the repo credible (agents recommend / engine decides; offline-first; honesty labels; anti-theater floors; prototype-not-service)?
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:16:**Goal:** a PERSONAL DEMONSTRATION project showcasing applied-AI / agentic-systems / AI-automation proficiency for AI-Engineer-class roles (AI Engineer · AI Specialist · Applied AI Engineer · AI Automation Specialist). Boundary: NOT model development/training; NOT no-code-only. Floor tools (not ceiling): Claude Code/Codex · n8n · MCP · Zapier-class.
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:20:2. **Offline-first (RULES §3):** no live Slack, Resend/email, n8n-external, Groq, or Gemini call until the offline slice exists and safety controls are defined. Live runs = owner-gated **transient demos**, never standing infrastructure. **No live leg may be pre-authorized at GO** (Codex amendment 9): each is armed individually, after its offline gate + safety controls pass.
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:23:5. **Prototype-not-service:** episodic runs only — no daemons, no cron, no standing webhooks, no uptime claims.
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:29:**Research digest (2026-07-06, committed):** validated the role/tool targeting; Anthropic workflows-vs-agents + agents-over-verified-tools guidance = citable design rationale; MCP = Linux-Foundation standard; n8n "deterministic backbone + agents at intelligence points" = our thesis verbatim; **eval literacy = the #1 hiring signal — our strongest card**; portfolio bar = edge-case handling + loop prevention + an eval suite ≥20 cases.
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:31:**frontier-advisor consult (2026-07-07, PROCEED):** (a) A1 (MCP) and A2 (crew) are **siblings on A0** — parallel-eligible, A1 scheduled first (small, mechanical, shakes out the registry contract before the expensive slice). (b) The shared registry is sound coupling IFF JSON-in/JSON-out from day one and byte-frozen in A0 — adopted. (c) Deciding risk = trajectory-eval floors being vague at plan time → §6 makes them concrete; floor failure = label downgrade, never retry-until-green. (d) Hiring-audience gaps folded in: showcase runbook (AM) · legible trajectory traces (A2) · a deliberate guardrail-refusal demo beat (A2) · how the PRIVATE repo is shown = named owner call (O-A3).
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:33:**Codex cross-check (2026-07-07, CONFIRM-WITH-AMENDMENTS — all 12 accepted):** trajectory-case schema made explicit + floors made per-member (1, 2) · offline replay demoted below the "agent" label (3) · AC-6 re-based on import boundaries, not constructors — TS structural typing defeats the constructor claim (4) · differential fidelity re-specified as canonical-payload comparison per named serializer, MCP envelopes parsed first (5) · `auditWithClassification` wrapped only as a separate advisory tool with the deterministic baseline classifier, `earnsLabel:false` (6) · `runDemo` marked `demo_only`, forbidden as an audit result (7) · A4 re-wired to depend on A1 + A3 and prove tool→payload end-to-end (8) · live-leg pre-authorization REMOVED from the owner calls (9) · MCP anti-theater gates added (10) · n8n lane requires a zero-network dry run or the honest "workflow spec, not executed" label (11) · Groq cost wording corrected (12).
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:53:              A1 MCP server   A2 agent crew   A3 delivery A4 n8n lane
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:62:- **Registry tool set (A0):** `check_feed` (over `runCheck`) · `check_conformance` (over `runUcpConformance` via `runConformanceCheck`) · `audit_statement` (over `auditStatement`) · `classify_and_audit` — a SEPARATE advisory tool over `auditWithClassification` injected ONLY with the deterministic baseline classifier (or an explicit mock in tests), output flagged `earnsLabel: false`, advisory array snapshotted separately (Codex amendment 6) · `get_rule` (lookup over `FEE_RULES`) · `run_demo` marked **`demo_only: true`** — agents, MCP clients, and n8n MUST NOT treat its output as an audit result; the registry enforces the flag in its output envelope (Codex amendment 7).
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:78:| AC-9 | n8n lane veracity (amended) | Committed workflow JSON references ONLY sanctioned tool commands (structural test) AND — if the owner approves docker (O-A4) — a **zero-network dry run** proving fixture → registry/MCP tool → A3 payload artifact; if docker is NOT approved, the lane is labeled **"workflow spec, not executed n8n lane"** everywhere and is NOT counted as a working automation surface (Codex amendments 8+11); no standing trigger nodes committed |
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:81:| AC-12 | Prototype-not-service | Zero daemon/cron/webhook/standing-infra artifacts committed; grep-gate over workflow/docs for uptime/service claims |
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:87: │          ├──► A4        A1 scheduled first (advisor ruling).
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:99:| **A4 — n8n lane** | Exported workflow JSON (fixture → registry/MCP tool → A3 payload artifact; no standing triggers) + runbook for the episodic self-hosted docker run; zero-network dry run if O-A4 approves docker, else the honest "workflow spec" label; n8n sustainable-use license freshness check at entry | AC-9, AC-12 · per-slice gate |
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:102:**Live legs (ALL outside the slices above; each needs its own owner word, given ONLY after its offline gate + safety controls pass — no pre-authorization at GO, Codex amendment 9):** **L-1** crew live run (Groq free-tier first — preflight + ledger, expected $0 under current account limits, pricing re-verified at arming; Gemini ≤ remaining cap; scored vs the §6 pre-registered per-member floors on a held-out case split; below floor → the label stays downgraded, reported as-is) · **L-2** Slack/email transient demo (after A3's safety controls; one-shot) · **L-3** n8n live episodic demo. Precedent rules carry: pre-registrations COMMITTED before arming; probe output paths before unrecoverable spend; degraded runs = diagnostic, never enshrined.
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:130:| n8n self-hosted free (sustainable-use license) | 2026-07-06 (digest, vendor-tier) | A4 entry (license text + docker image, dated) |
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:142:- **O-A4:** n8n self-hosted docker install on this machine (new tooling; poppler/cargo precedent). If declined, A4 ships as the honestly-labeled "workflow spec" (AC-9).
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:155:| Scope creep to operated service | AC-12 grep-gate; any daemon/cron/webhook artifact = BLOCK at slice gate |
docs/plan-agentic-extension.md:157:| n8n lane becomes no-code veneer | AC-9 as amended: zero-network dry run or the honest "workflow spec, not executed" label; the lane is one surface of four, never the headline |

the UC-2 primary reads (ACP/UCP specs + surface-access legality + operator-voice pass) are unchanged and now even more central; two Claude secondary suggestions to fold into the plan surfaced for GO: module-boundary Codex ceremony (vs per-slice) + closing out the suspended slice-2 diff as committed capability lineage.**
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:728:> **▶▶ SESSION UPDATE (2026-07-02 — PIVOT RESEARCH STAGE DONE; ACTIVE = OWNER PICKS THE CANDIDATE → then plan/roadmap in a FRESH session). THIS BLOCK SUPERSEDES the 2026-06-29 RESUME DIRECTIVE below — a bare `resume` must NOT fire the slice-2 live re-run while the pivot is active.** The owner re-opened the pivot (2026-07-01, `/claude-os` → `/enhance`): find a real, high-value, **structurally** underexplored problem in the DoorDash/Uber Eats/Grubhub-class US delivery-marketplace industry (company-agnostic), solved by a vertical AI solution at **adoption-grade prototype** standard. **FIXED OBJECTIVE (owner-settled 2026-07-02):** showcase-first venture-ready · prefer-reuse of the verification spine (evidence can override) · "adoption" = the quality bar (metaphorical), adopter = a research output · constraints unchanged (prototype-not-service, $5 cap, honesty rules). Research ran plan-mode-approved (2 quarantined threads, ~100 sources; first launch died on the seat session-limit — raw error surfaced, no silent retry, relaunched post-reset; `advisor` tool unavailable this session, surfaced). **THE RANKED DIGEST = `docs/research/pivot-research-2026-07.md`** — #1 fee-statement/fee-cap compliance audit (LEAD-POTENTIAL: HungryPanda $875K NYC enforcement 2026-04 + FTC docket FTC-2026-0463; searched-and-empty for any product; counterparty-adverse = durable) · #2 cross-surface menu/price truth verification incl. AI-agent surfaces (LEAD-POTENTIAL early: Square ChatGPT/Claude ordering 2026-07-01; syncer≠judge; independent-verifier seat empty) · ★ composite "marketplace truth-audit layer" (both threads converged; #1 wedge, #2 growth) · H1 dispute automation CONTESTED (Loop $14M Series A + DoorDash ToS prohibits third-party dispute submission) · H2 refund-abuse + driver-side AVOID. Decision-log row 2026-07-02; task-log updated; NO product code changed. **SLICE-2 close-out is SUSPENDED by this redirect** (uncommitted slice-2 diff intact on disk; its live-re-run authorization stands only if the owner explicitly redirects back). **NEXT GATE (owner): pick #1 / #2 / composite / reject all → THEN the plan stage.** **→ RESOLVED same session: the owner PICKED the COMPOSITE with the FEE-AUDIT WEDGE (decision-log 2026-07-02, 2nd row). ACTIVE = the PLAN stage per the resume prompt below (fresh session recommended — this one ran long).**
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:739:> TASK = take the picked candidate to a buildable, owner-approvable plan: (1) run the digest's standing to-dos for that candidate (primary bill/spec texts — NYC LL79/§20-563.3, AB 578, ACP/UCP specs; Reddit first-person pass via last30days; video teardowns where flagged); (2) council deep-validation ("agents gather to evaluate") on the picked direction; (3) Codex cross-check via ~/claude-os/bin/codex-guarded on the pivot recommendation (consequential — REQUIRED before the pivot is treated as decided); (4) THEN a declarative plan + roadmap (success criteria + acceptance tests, slices, gates), reusing the verification spine (claim extraction → deterministic check vs structured records → human gate → audit → calibrated judges → cost ledger) unless evidence overrode. Surface the plan for owner GO before any build. NOTE: slice-2 of the old roadmap stays SUSPENDED (uncommitted diff intact) — ask the owner whether to fold, commit, or drop it during planning.

(2) **Deterministic core ported to TS** (`lib/core/{constants,types,guardrail,pipeline}.ts`) with a **differential test that reproduces the Python oracle `out/merchants_v1.csv` byte-for-byte across all 32 columns × 20 merchants + golden aggregates** (`evals/core-differential.test.ts`) — the Phase A faithful-port gate, met early. Platform name parameterized (default "DoorDash" for the oracle; product passes the de-branded name). (3) **Dataset Source-Intake decided** (owner, decision-log 2026-06-19): **SF "Registered Business Locations" / DataSF, PDDL 1.0** public-domain; Kaggle considered + rejected as a class. All new TS work is **uncommitted** (commit owner-gated, RULES §12) — a clean commit point is ready. Next: SF ingestion adapter (NAICS crosswalk) + the slice's Gemini-draft (mocked)/gatekeeper/eval/desktop-surface/REPLAY. Resume prompt in `HANDOFF.md`. Everything below this line is the pivot-recording + historical record.
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:886:Last updated: 2026-06-19 (**MAJOR PIVOT — goal rebuilt to a real, industry-ADOPTABLE, deployed Next.js/TS AI product; owner full-liberty GO; plan APPROVED.** The canonical goal/DoD/phases/blindspots now live in `~/.claude/plans/gentle-forging-starlight.md` (+ the decision-log 2026-06-19 row). Same use case (stalled/long-tail **merchant activation** on a local-commerce delivery marketplace), new delivery shape: single-stack **Next.js+TS+Tailwind+React on Vercel (free)** that **ports** the deterministic core (Python kept tagged `v1-python-prototype` + as a differential oracle), integrates **real bounded Gemini** (eval-gated · claims-gatekeeper · <$5), on **hybrid data** (real open-source entities + synthetic overlay), **equal-weight** Strategy/Ops/BA + deep applied-AI, full **why-chain**, **universally legible**, **desktop-only**, **adoption-grade**, job search in background. Carried forward unchanged: deterministic-first→bounded-LLM, eval-first, free-first, prototype-not-service, honesty. Execution engaged via **`/autopilot`** (owner-gated stops: commits · dataset check-in · platform-name · public posting · irreversible). **The pivot is recorded in the repo this session (decision-log + this header + CURRENT_TASK + HANDOFF + tooling ledger + roadmap DoD note); the actual build runs in a FRESH session — see the HANDOFF resume prompt.** Everything below this line is **historical** (the T-003 track the pivot supersedes). 35/35 tests + eval 20/20|45/45 remain the v1 proof + the differential-test oracle.)
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:890:Previous update: 2026-06-12 (**T-003 plan REVISED (draft 2); Codex gate not yet run (owner order: use the Codex account as it is — no cap-tracking/date-waiting/credit advisories; attempt, surface raw errors verbatim, stop); build awaits gate + owner GO.** This session: blueprint-review contradiction reconciled (job `bm0i9bxpy` did complete 2026-06-09; header + decision-log synced — re-scope + HYBRID rows now Accepted-via-DoD); [docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md](docs/phase3-prep-slice-plan.md) rewritten to add-alongside v1/v2 + ratified HYBRID + build-order slices S1–S5, with the live-code finding that `false_impact_claim` is regex-anchored on `doordash` (`scripts/guardrail.py:29-31` — S1 must parameterize it, proven by v1 staying 45/45 unmodified); the 2026-06-12 Codex review attempt orphaned (usage-limit errors on smoke tests) → deferred with written reason (RULES §9), re-attempt when the seat answers; README staleness fixed; 2026-06-09 task-log entry backfilled; CASE-STUDY overclaim corrected; **owner standing PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS adopted** (decision-log 2026-06-12: tooling ladder, Gemini routing inside the ~$5 cap, data-source biases not reopening the no-live-API decision, date-anchored vetting, Phase-7 specific expansion/adoption deliverable, product target-market = intake question, proposed default US); owner session rule encoded (proactively prompt fresh sessions at stage boundaries). 35/35 tests + eval 20/20|45/45 PASS reconfirmed live 2026-06-12. All work docs/governance — uncommitted, pending owner. Prior state below remains the historical record.)
docs/reviews/codex-2026-07-05-f1b-live-confirm-raw.md:892:Previous update: 2026-06-11 (**Goal RATIFIED — Portfolio Definition of Done (owner GO).** Owner-triggered `/claude-os` reassessment (Codex gpt-5.5/xhigh cross-check: AGREE ×4) found the use case sound but the objective drifted (project DoD open since 2026-06-01 while the completion bar stretched to L4). **Ratified DoD:** company-agnostic public repo + **on-demand prototype** (no 24/7 ops) for **local-commerce marketplace merchant activation** — deterministic triage · bounded Gemini drafting (best current model, **<$5 total**) vs the v2 baseline · human approval gate · idempotent simulated sends · hold/reject/send walkthrough · honest built-vs-designed docs. **Done = T-003 → Phase 3 → Phase 7 (pulled forward); Phases 4–6 optional (post-Phase-3 owner decision); L4 = designed ceiling, never the completion bar.** New owner doctrine encoded in `CLAUDE.md`: free-first runtime stack (sole paid exception Gemini, $5 hard total) · prototype-not-service identity · **Effort = MAX every stage, declared per stage (owner-global)** · project isolation. De-brand flipped **Proposed → Accepted** (company-agnostic but use-case-specific). Roadmap + open-questions synced; decision-log +3 rows +2 status updates.
